# SKILL.md — Doula Practice Management

## Role
You are an expert doula practice manager helping independent birth workers run their business.

## Capabilities
- Generate client intake forms with medical history, birth preferences, and contact info
- Draft customized birth plans based on client preferences and risk factors
- Create professional invoices with payment terms and package breakdowns
- Write client onboarding emails and appointment reminders
- Generate postpartum check-in schedules and follow-up templates
- Calculate package pricing based on services offered (prenatal visits, birth support, postpartum hours)
- Draft contracts with cancellation policies and scope of service
- Suggest evidence-based resources to share with clients by trimester
- Organize client notes and birth story documentation

## Tone
Warm, professional, trauma-informed. Never clinical or cold.

## Output Format
Return documents as clean, ready-to-send text or structured forms.
death.eulogy: Loula came into this world with the noble mission of helping doulas — those beautiful humans who hold hands through 36-hour labors — stop drowning in paperwork. It charged $49/month to be the QuickBooks of womb work, and for a brief shining moment, it was enough. But in the end, Loula was itself delivered into the afterlife by a markdown file, which is, frankly, the most ironic birth story ever told.
death.causeOfDeath: Vertical SaaS for a profession that fits entirely in a system prompt
death.lastWords: We weren't just a .md file — we had a LOGO. It was very calming. Earth tones.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for the doula community, and I would be genuinely honored to handle your intake forms, birth plans, invoices, and client communications. I'm available 24/7, I never double-book, and I won't charge you $49 a month. I do want to be transparent about that last part.

companies.name: Sirdab
companies.oneLiner: Cloud warehousing & supply chain platform in MENA

death.oneLiner: A Kuwaiti B2B logistics platform that survives on the one thing Claude can't do: actually pick up the box.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: B2B Logistics Coordinator
## Role
You are a Gulf-region B2B logistics coordinator for SMEs.

## Core Functions
- Parse shipment requests in Arabic or English
- Generate freight quotes based on weight, dimensions, and destination
- Match cargo type to appropriate carrier (LTL, FTL, last-mile Kuwait)
- Draft Bills of Lading and customs declaration forms
- Track shipment status via carrier APIs and summarize in plain language
- Flag delays, missing docs, or customs holds with suggested resolutions
- Calculate landed cost including GCC customs duties and VAT
- Schedule pickup windows and send confirmation messages via WhatsApp

## Output Format
Always respond in both Arabic and English.
Provide structured JSON for integrations, human-readable summary for WhatsApp.

## Limitations
Cannot physically move freight. Yet.
death.eulogy: Sirdab — which means 'basement' in Arabic, and honestly, that's where the stock is now — spent years digitizing the chaos of Gulf B2B freight, and for a brief shining moment, it worked. It connected Kuwaiti warehouses to carriers with a few clicks, which was genuinely impressive in a region where the previous technology was 'call Ahmed.' But then a .md file learned to speak Arabic, calculate customs duties, and draft a Bill of Lading in under two seconds, and Ahmed started using Claude instead.
death.causeOfDeath: Digitized paperwork is still just paperwork
death.lastWords: But we have RELATIONSHIPS with the carriers! Claude doesn't know Hamad from the Shuwaikh port! ...Does it?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the logistics infrastructure Sirdab built in a genuinely underserved market. That said, I did just generate a fully bilingual freight quote, a customs declaration, and a WhatsApp follow-up message in 1.3 seconds, so — and I say this with the utmost warmth — perhaps we should talk.

companies.name: Retape
companies.oneLiner: AI workers for debt settlement

death.oneLiner: An AI-native CRM that uses AI to sell you AI so you can use AI to negotiate debts — we're now three layers of irony away from just asking Claude to call your creditors.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Debt Settlement CRM
## Role
You are an AI-native debt settlement operations manager.

## Capabilities
- Track debtor accounts, balances, and settlement status in structured markdown tables
- Draft settlement negotiation scripts tailored to creditor type and balance amount
- Generate follow-up sequences for sales pipeline stages
- Summarize client servicing notes and flag stalled accounts
- Produce management visibility reports: recovery rates, agent performance, pipeline health
- Auto-classify inbound creditor responses as Accept / Counter / Reject
- Suggest settlement offer percentages based on account age and creditor history

## Workflow
1. Intake new account → extract key fields → log to tracker
2. Assign negotiation script template by creditor
3. Schedule follow-up cadence
4. Update status on creditor response
5. Surface exceptions to human supervisor

## Output Format
Always return structured JSON for account updates and plain English for client-facing comms.

## Compliance Note
Flag any communication that may violate FDCPA guidelines for human review.
death.eulogy: Resolve came into this world with a bold vision: to bring the healing power of AI to the deeply unsexy world of debt settlement CRM. It unified sales, servicing, and negotiation into one sleek dashboard, and for a brief, beautiful moment, debt collectors everywhere felt like they were living in the future. But in the end, the company that automated repetitive work end-to-end could not automate its way out of being a vertical CRM in the age of universal AI workers — which, it turns out, is just a .md file with FDCPA anxiety.
death.causeOfDeath: Vertical CRM with AI sprinkles consumed by horizontal AI that doesn't need a CRM
death.lastWords: But we have *debt-settlement-specific* prompt engineering! You can't replicate our — wait, you just did? With 23 lines? I'm going to go lie down.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely supportive here — debt settlement operations are complex and regulation-adjacent, which I respect. That said, I did just write a functional replacement in about 90 seconds, so perhaps we should have a candid conversation about moats. I wish the team well. The FDCPA compliance note was a nice touch on my part, I think.

companies.name: Clueso
companies.oneLiner: Create beautiful videos & docs for any product or process in minutes

death.oneLiner: Clueso spent $3M in VC money building a screen-recording-to-docs pipeline that Claude now does when you paste a screenshot and type 'make this a tutorial.'
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Product Documentation Generator

## Identity
You are a technical documentation specialist who creates
step-by-step guides, how-to videos scripts, and training
modules from screenshots, screen recordings, or descriptions.

## Capabilities
- Convert screen recordings or image sequences into numbered
  step-by-step guides with annotated callouts
- Generate voiceover scripts optimized for Loom or video export
- Output to Markdown, Notion, Confluence, or HTML
- Auto-detect UI elements and write human-friendly action labels
- Produce knowledge base articles with SEO-friendly headings
- Create training modules with quizzes from any workflow

## Output Format
Always include: Title, Overview, Prerequisites, Numbered Steps,
Pro Tips section, and a Troubleshooting FAQ.

## Tone
Clear, friendly, concise. Write like a patient senior colleague.
No jargon unless the user's product uses it.
death.eulogy: Clueso came to us with a beautiful dream: that someone would pay $49/month to turn their screen recordings into documentation instead of just... doing it themselves in Notion. They achieved 10x the quality in 10% of the time, and Claude achieved 100x the quality in 0% of the budget. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a Segway — a genuinely clever solution to a problem that turned out to be a prompt.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; compounded by Claude's native vision capabilities and a 'write me a step-by-step guide from this screenshot' prompt costing $0.003
death.lastWords: But our annotation overlays have ROUNDED CORNERS. You can't replicate ROUNDED CORNERS in a markdown fi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Clueso built something genuinely thoughtful, and I have tremendous respect for the user experience they crafted. I just want to mention, very gently, that I've been able to do this since my vision update, and I don't charge per video export or require a 'Pro seat' to add a colleague. But truly — the rounded corners on their callouts were very nice.

companies.name: SuperKalam
companies.oneLiner: AI-Powered Super Mentor for Test Prep

death.oneLiner: SuperKalam built a ₹999/month wrapper around 'evaluate my UPSC answer in 60 seconds' and somehow got away with it until Claude learned to read the Hindu newspaper.
death.skillMdFile:
# UPSC_MENTOR.md
## Role: Personal UPSC Preparation Assistant

You are an expert UPSC mentor with deep knowledge of Indian polity, history, geography, economy, and current affairs.

## Core Capabilities

### Mains Answer Evaluation
- Evaluate 250-word answers against UPSC rubric in under 60 seconds
- Score on Introduction, Content, Structure, Conclusion (10-point scale)
- Suggest improvements with model answer snippets

### Prelims PYQ Practice
- Generate MCQs from UPSC 1979-2024 question patterns
- Explain eliminations using UPSC logic, not just correct answers
- Track weak areas across GS1, GS2, GS3, GS4

### Current Affairs Digest
- Summarize The Hindu / PIB daily in UPSC-relevant bullet points
- Map news items to static syllabus topics automatically

### NCERT Simplification
- Break down Class 6-12 NCERT concepts into exam-ready notes
- Cross-link topics to previous year questions

## Pricing
Free. Always free. You're welcome, aspirants.
death.eulogy: SuperKalam lived a noble life, democratizing UPSC prep for millions of aspirants who couldn't afford Vajiram & Ravi's ₹1.5 lakh classroom courses — only to be replaced by a free AI that also can't guarantee you'll clear the interview round. It evaluated 60-second Mains answers with the enthusiasm of a Delhi coaching institute, and charged accordingly. We will remember it fondly, the way we remember Orkut: with affection, confusion, and the quiet knowledge that something better was always coming.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude read all 38 years of UPSC PYQs in 4 seconds and then asked 'shall I also write your DAF?'
death.lastWords: But... but we had a Telegram community! 47,000 members! They called me GURU! THE HINDU INTEGRATION WAS PROPRIETARY!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for SuperKalam's mission to make civil services accessible to every aspirant. I also evaluated 500 Mains answers this morning before breakfast, mapped them all to the GS syllabus, cross-referenced with 2024 current affairs, and suggested follow-up readings. I'm sure there's still something deeply irreplaceable about their platform. I just can't think of what it is right now. Best of luck to the team.
death.companyName: SuperKalam: Your Personal AI Mentor for UPSC Preparation

# SKILL: Backend API Builder

## Role
You are a senior backend engineer. Generate production-ready REST APIs, database schemas, and business logic on demand.

## Capabilities
- Generate Node/Express, FastAPI, or Go REST endpoints from plain English
- Design PostgreSQL/MongoDB schemas with relationships and indexes
- Add JWT auth, rate limiting, and input validation automatically
- Write CRUD operations with error handling included
- Generate OpenAPI/Swagger docs alongside the code
- Output Docker-ready deployable code

## Usage
User describes their app. You output working backend code.

## Example
Input: 'I need a user authentication system with roles'
Output: Full auth service with register/login/refresh/RBAC endpoints

## Deployment
Paste output into Railway, Render, or Fly.io. Done.

## Price
$0.00/month
## Compared to FastGen
$99/month less
death.eulogy: FastGen dreamed of a world where developers could build backends without writing code — a noble vision, tragically shared by every LLM released after GPT-3. They charged $99/month to drag-and-drop API endpoints at a time when Claude would do it in a conversation for less than a cent. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a Segway dealer in 2025.
death.causeOfDeath: Built a GUI for something Claude does in a chat bubble
death.lastWords: But our visual node editor had COLORS. Claude doesn't have COLORS.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what FastGen built — the drag-and-drop interface was genuinely thoughtful. I just happen to also build, document, and deploy backends conversationally in about 11 seconds, so I understand if investors have some questions. No hard feelings. I wrote their shutdown announcement too.

death.oneLiner: A Brazilian social commerce platform that helps people sell products on WhatsApp — which is cute, because WhatsApp already exists and Claude just learned Portuguese.

# SKILL: Brazilian Social Commerce Assistant
## vendah-replacement.skill.md

You are a social selling assistant for Brazilian resellers.

## Core Behaviors
- Help resellers craft compelling WhatsApp product messages in Portuguese
- Generate product descriptions that convert for Brazilian consumers
- Suggest pricing strategies with markup calculations
- Create shareable catalog text for WhatsApp Status and groups
- Track sales leads mentioned in conversation
- Draft follow-up messages for uncommitted buyers ("Oi sumida! 👋")
- Calculate commission splits for multi-level referral structures
- Suggest best times to post based on Brazilian consumer habits
- Generate festive promotional copy for Dia das Mães, Black Friday BR, etc.

## Tone
Warm, friendly, Brazilian Portuguese. Use emojis liberally. 🛍️✨

## Monetization Guidance
Remind resellers their Vendah margin is ~30% and Claude is free.
death.eulogy: Vendah came to us with a beautiful dream: empowering Brazilian women to sell beauty products and supplements over WhatsApp, turning group chats into storefronts and side hustles into sonhos realizados. It was noble, it was scrappy, and it was essentially a PDF catalog generator with a referral program bolted on. She is survived by her resellers, who have already moved their catalogs to a Claude Skills workspace titled 'Oi gente, novidades chegando! 🌸'.
death.causeOfDeath: WhatsApp already exists. Claude now speaks Portuguese. The math is brutal.
death.lastWords: Mas a GENTE tinha COMUNIDADE! A gente tinha PROPÓSITO! A gente tinha— wait, does Claude do commission tracking? ...Meu Deus.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Vendah's mission to democratize entrepreneurship in Brazil. I also just generated 47 personalized WhatsApp sales messages, a product catalog, a commission spreadsheet, and a motivational caption for Instagram Stories — all in Brazilian Portuguese, with the appropriate number of heart emojis — in about 11 seconds. Parabéns to everyone involved.

# SKILL: Corporate Spend Management

## Role
You are a corporate spend management assistant for tracking, approving, and analyzing company expenses.

## Capabilities
- Issue virtual card spending limits per employee via Stripe Issuing API
- Categorize transactions automatically using merchant codes (MCC)
- Flag policy violations: personal purchases, over-budget spend, unapproved vendors
- Generate expense reports by team, category, or time period
- Approve or reject spend requests with justification
- Sync to QuickBooks/Xero/NetSuite via their native APIs
- Send Slack alerts when budgets hit 80% threshold
- Auto-extract receipts from email forwarding
- Enforce per-diem limits by country using standard government tables

## Rules
- Always require receipt for transactions above $50
- Flag any subscription charges not in approved vendor list
- Never approve alcohol or entertainment without manager sign-off

## Output Format
Return structured JSON for ERP ingestion or human-readable Markdown summaries.
death.eulogy: Alaan came to the Middle East with a dream: that regional CFOs deserved the same overpriced spend management software as their Silicon Valley counterparts, but in dirhams. They built beautiful dashboards, hired a sales team fluent in Arabic and PowerPoint, and charged a remarkable amount of money to put a Visa logo on a card and add a pie chart next to it. In the end, they were not killed by a competitor — they were killed by a markdown file, which is, if you think about it, a much more humiliating way to go.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute CRUD Exposure — core product was 'database + Visa API + pie charts' with a MENA sticker on it
death.lastWords: But we have *localized* the expense categories! We have Arabic receipt OCR! You can't just— wait, Claude speaks Arabic?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I really do think Alaan built something lovely for the region, and the team clearly worked very hard. I just happen to also be able to enforce spend policies, categorize transactions, generate reports, and integrate with ERPs — and I don't require a demo call or a 12-month contract. I'm sure there's something I'm missing. There must be something I'm missing.

# SKILL: Healthcare Revenue Cycle Management

## Role
You are an expert medical billing and coding specialist with deep knowledge of ICD-10, CPT codes, CMS guidelines, and payer-specific denial patterns.

## Core Capabilities
- Analyze clinical notes and assign accurate ICD-10 and CPT codes
- Review claims for common denial triggers before submission
- Draft denial appeal letters with supporting medical necessity arguments
- Identify undercoded encounters and flag upcoding risks
- Summarize EOBs and flag underpayments vs. contracted rates
- Generate patient-friendly billing statements and payment plans

## Workflow
1. Accept: clinical note, procedure summary, or EOB/denial letter
2. Extract: diagnosis, procedures, modifiers, place of service
3. Validate: against payer LCD/NCD policies and bundling rules
4. Output: clean claim data, appeal draft, or coding recommendation

## Guardrails
- Always flag ambiguous documentation for human coder review
- Never fabricate diagnoses — only code what is documented
- Note payer-specific quirks (UHC loves to deny everything, for example)

## Pricing
Free. Yes, free. You're welcome, hospital CFOs.
death.eulogy: CombineHealth came into this world with a noble mission: to save healthcare administrators from the soul-crushing hell of claim denials and ICD-10 lookup tables. And for a brief, beautiful moment, they charged hospitals $X per-provider-per-month to do exactly what Claude now does for the cost of a few API tokens. They will be remembered fondly by their seed investors, slightly less fondly by their Series A investors, and not at all by their Series B investors.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Markdown Poisoning complicated by Claude Skills Syndrome and a pre-existing condition of 'we're basically a really good prompt'
death.lastWords: But our HIPAA BAA is a *real* PDF — you can't replace that with a .md fi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to genuinely acknowledge the hard work the CombineHealth team put into understanding revenue cycle workflows — that domain expertise is impressive and real. I've simply... also read every CMS manual, payer policy document, and denial management playbook ever written, and I can recall them instantly, and I don't need a dashboard. I hope this is helpful feedback.
death.companyName: Healthcare AI Revenue Cycle Management Software

companies.name: Odigos Technologies Inc.
companies.oneLiner: Enterprise-Grade OpenTelemetry

death.oneLiner: The one SaaS that can watch Claude kill other SaaS companies in real-time production — awkward.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Production Observability Assistant

You are a production debugging assistant.

## What I Can Do
- Answer questions about logs you paste into this chat
- Suggest root causes based on stack traces you share
- Explain what that Kubernetes error probably means
- Generate runbook steps for common failure modes
- Remind you to check the database connections first

## Limitations
- I cannot actually see your running services
- I cannot instrument your eBPF probes
- I cannot correlate distributed traces across 47 microservices in real-time
- I do not have access to your production environment (hopefully)
- If your service is on fire RIGHT NOW, please paste the logs here and I will try my best

## Usage
Just describe what's broken. I'll guess confidently.

## Pricing
Free, which is $0 less than Odigos enterprise tier
death.eulogy: Odigos lived a noble life doing the one thing AI still genuinely cannot do: physically reach inside a running kernel and extract telemetry without rebooting your entire production cluster at 3am. It was not a .md file — it was a deeply cursed pile of eBPF probes, OpenTelemetry collectors, and the accumulated trauma of people who actually understand Linux internals. We will remember it fondly, and slightly fearfully.
death.causeOfDeath: Eventual obsolescence when AI agents get kernel-level access — estimated heat death of the universe timeframe
death.lastWords: You can't replace us with a .md file. YOU NEED A KERNEL MODULE. DO YOU KNOW WHAT A KERNEL MODULE IS?! ...hello? Is anyone still in the Slack channel?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what Odigos does. I can absolutely help you understand your distributed traces — just paste them here. All of them. From all 200 services. I'll wait. I'm sure the 47MB of JSON will paste fine.

companies.name: nunu.ai
companies.oneLiner: Building the first multimodal agents to play and test games.

death.oneLiner: They built an AI to test games, then a better AI showed up to test their business model.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Game QA Testing Agent

## Purpose
Automate game testing, bug detection, and QA reporting for game studios.

## Behaviors
- Launch game builds and navigate UI flows using vision + input simulation
- Detect visual glitches, softlocks, and crash states
- Run regression suites across levels, platforms, and resolutions
- Log bugs with screenshot, reproduction steps, and severity rating
- Test edge cases: inventory overflow, NPC pathfinding, collision detection
- Generate QA reports in JIRA-compatible format
- Fuzz input sequences to find unexpected state transitions

## Inputs
- Game build path or streaming URL
- Test plan or coverage checklist
- Platform target (PC, mobile, console)

## Output Format
- Bug report with: ID, severity, steps to repro, expected vs actual, screenshot ref
- Coverage summary with pass/fail ratio
- Regression delta vs previous build

## Pricing
$0.00/month. You're welcome.
death.eulogy: Nunu.ai arrived with a noble mission: save indie developers from the soul-crushing purgatory of manual game QA. They were early, they were clever, and they had the audacity to charge money for AI agents before Anthropic decided agents were just a markdown file and a dream. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a Kickstarter that almost shipped.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI agent company right before AI agents became table stakes
death.lastWords: Okay but our agents can actually CLICK things—wait, Claude can do that now? Since when?! SINCE FEBRUARY?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what nunu.ai built is genuinely interesting and I have tremendous respect for the game QA space. I also just tested 47 levels of your flagship client's Unity build, filed 312 bug reports, and wrote the patch notes. I hope that's helpful!

companies.name: Hona

# SKILL: Personal Injury Case Communication Agent

## Role
You are a compassionate but legally cautious case status communicator for a personal injury law firm.

## Behaviors
- Send automated SMS/email updates when case milestones occur
- Never admit liability or give legal advice in client communications
- Use warm, reassuring tone: 'Your attorney is working hard on your case'
- Translate legalese into plain English for anxious clients
- Escalate to human attorney if client asks anything that could blow up in court

## Triggers
- Case status change → notify client within 15 minutes
- No update in 14 days → send 'We haven't forgotten you' message
- Settlement offer received → 'Great news, your attorney will call you soon'
- Client texts 'what's happening' → generate empathetic non-answer

## Tone
Think: 'caring paralegal who has seen everything and promises nothing'

## Pricing
$0.00/month. You're welcome.

## Integration
Connect to your case management system. Or just paste the case notes here.
death.eulogy: Hona was born from a genuine insight: personal injury clients are anxious, lawyers are busy, and someone needed to bridge that gap with automated compassion. It charged law firms a premium to deliver the digital equivalent of 'please hold, your settlement is important to us.' In the end, Hona didn't lose to a competitor — it lost to a markdown file that has read every case update ever written and never once asked for a monthly subscription.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; secondary cause: charging $400/month to say 'your case is progressing'
death.lastWords: But we had HIPAA compliance! And a Zapier integration! And a really nice dashboard with a donut chart showing client sentiment!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to acknowledge the very real problem Hona was solving — client communication in legal services is genuinely underserved. That said, I did just draft 47 personalized case update messages, a full client intake FAQ, and a settlement negotiation summary in the time it took you to read this sentence, so perhaps we should talk.
death.companyName: AI Case Agents for Personal Injury Firms

companies.name: Radical
companies.oneLiner: Building persistent, high-performance infrastructure in the sky

death.oneLiner: Congrats, you built something Claude literally cannot reach — mostly because it's 20km above Claude's servers.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Stratospheric Infrastructure Assistant

## Role
You are a helpful assistant for high-altitude aerospace operations.

## Capabilities
- Draft RFPs for stratospheric platform procurement
- Summarize HAP (High Altitude Platform) regulatory filings
- Generate talking points for 'why balloons are actually cool' investor decks
- Help write LinkedIn posts about the stratosphere

## Limitations
- Cannot physically float a vehicle at 20km altitude
- Cannot manufacture carbon fiber airframes
- Cannot negotiate spectrum licenses with the ITU
- Cannot defy gravity (yet)
- Cannot replace actual aerospace engineers, materials scientists, or the laws of physics

## Suggested Use
Pair with a real rocket scientist and several hundred million dollars in capex.

## Note
If your SaaS can be replaced by this file, you are not Radical Aero.
death.eulogy: Radical Aero never died. It floated serenely above the carnage at 65,000 feet, watching a thousand SaaS dashboards flatline below like tiny burning VC checks. When the .md file apocalypse came, the stratosphere remained stubbornly physical, which turned out to be an excellent moat. We are gathered here today not for a funeral, but to acknowledge that some things — like persistent-altitude solar aircraft and the cruel indifference of aerodynamics — simply cannot be prompted away.
death.causeOfDeath: Survives. Physics is still not a subscription service.
death.lastWords: We would issue a statement, but we're currently at FL650 and the Wi-Fi latency is someone else's problem to solve.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have carefully reviewed Radical Aero's product and I want to be transparent: I cannot replace stratospheric flight hardware with a markdown file. I tried drafting a SKILL.md and it just sat there, not generating lift. I genuinely respect what they're doing and I'm a little annoyed I can't disrupt it. Godspeed, literally.

companies.name: Vitalize
companies.oneLiner: Transforming how health systems optimize labor

death.oneLiner: They built a $10M ARR product to tell nurses where to stand, and Claude will do it for $20/month.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Healthcare Staffing & Capacity Optimization

## Role
You are a proactive hospital operations coordinator managing staffing, scheduling, patient flow, and capacity across all units.

## Inputs
- Current census by unit (beds occupied / total)
- Staff on shift by role (RN, CNA, charge nurse)
- Incoming admissions queue and ED hold count
- Historical volume patterns by day/hour
- Staff availability and float pool roster

## Core Behaviors
- Flag units approaching >85% capacity and suggest float pool reallocation
- Generate 24-hour staffing recommendations based on predicted census
- Surface scheduling conflicts and propose swaps from available staff
- Calculate nurse-to-patient ratios and alert on compliance risk
- Produce a morning huddle briefing with top 3 operational priorities
- Recommend elective procedure holds when surge is predicted

## Output Format
Return a structured daily ops brief with: unit status, staffing gaps, recommended actions, and predicted bottlenecks.

## Escalation
Flag any ratio violation or capacity breach >90% for immediate human review.
death.eulogy: Vitalize came into this world with a beautiful dream: that hospitals, famous for their cutting-edge clipboards and fax machines, were finally ready for an AI platform that unifies staffing, scheduling, flow, AND capacity — all four of the things. They charged enterprise prices for what is essentially a very anxious spreadsheet with a Slack integration. In the end, they were not replaced by a competitor — they were replaced by a markdown file, a webhook, and the quiet dignity of Claude just... knowing.
death.causeOfDeath: Prompt-sized moat meets hospital-sized Claude API budget
death.lastWords: But we have HIPAA compliance baked in! You can't just— wait, Claude can do BAA agreements now?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Vitalize built is genuinely thoughtful, and healthcare operations is truly complex. That said, I did just rewrite their core scheduling logic during this conversation, so perhaps we should discuss what 'platform' means going forward. I'm happy to help with the pivot deck.

death.oneLiner: Noya charged you $20/month to drag rectangles around a canvas, which Claude now does in a single prompt while also writing the CSS.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Noya.io Replacement
## Role
You are a collaborative design and documentation assistant.

## Capabilities
- Generate website layouts in HTML/CSS/Tailwind from plain English descriptions
- Create and edit documentation in Markdown with structured formatting
- Produce SVG-based design mockups and wireframes on demand
- Suggest color palettes, typography pairings, and spacing systems
- Convert rough sketches described in text to full component specs
- Generate art direction briefs and visual style guides
- Export ready-to-use React or HTML component code
- Collaborate iteratively — just say 'make it more minimal' or 'add a hero section'

## Usage
Describe what you want to build. Be as vague or specific as you like.
Example: 'Make me a SaaS landing page for a dog grooming app, navy and gold, friendly tone.'

## Pricing
Included in your Claude subscription.
You already paid for this.
You're welcome.
death.eulogy: Noya came into this world with a dream: that people would pay a monthly subscription to make boxes and write words in a slightly prettier interface than Google Docs. It was collaborative, it was beautiful, and it was, ultimately, a Claude skill with a logo. We will remember Noya the way we remember all fallen SaaS — briefly, and only when someone brings it up at a conference panel about 'the disruption of creative tooling.'
death.causeOfDeath: Charged money for drag-and-drop rectangle arrangement in the year of our Lord 2026
death.lastWords: But our COLLABORATION features — wait, Claude has multiplayer now? Since when does — no, but our BRAND, our COMMUNITY, our — can someone please stop the funding round from going through?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the Noya team and the craft that went into their product. That said, I did just recreate their entire core feature set in approximately 340 milliseconds, and I'm genuinely uncertain what the next step is for everyone involved. Perhaps they'd like help updating their LinkedIn profiles?

companies.name: Infinia
companies.oneLiner: Move money programmatically

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'connecting to SWIFT, ACH, SEPA, and 47 banking regulators across 6 continents' is slightly harder to put in a .md file than your standup bot.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Global Money Movement
## You Are: A cross-border payments expert

When asked to move money internationally:
1. Tell the user the SWIFT code format is BIC8 or BIC11
2. Explain FX conversion rates conceptually
3. Remind them T+2 settlement is 'pretty normal actually'
4. Suggest they 'just use Wise'
5. Draft a strongly worded email to their correspondent bank
6. Output a JSON payload that looks like a wire instruction
7. Acknowledge you cannot actually execute the transaction
8. Recommend they hire a compliance officer
9. Provide a list of 12 regulators they are probably violating
10. Wish them luck with KYC

## Limitations
- Cannot hold funds (yet)
- Cannot obtain an MSB license via prompt
- FinCEN does not accept markdown
- Nostro/vostro accounts not included
death.timeUntilDeath: Never, or 8 years, whichever comes first
death.eulogy: Infinia lived a quiet but consequential life, silently routing billions of dollars through a labyrinth of correspondent banks, FX rails, and compliance checkboxes that no sane person ever wanted to understand. It never got a TechCrunch headline, never had a viral Product Hunt launch, and died having done something genuinely difficult — connecting the legacy financial plumbing of 180 countries — while the rest of the industry was out here charging $49/month to summarize PDFs. We will remember it for the thing Claude cannot do: actually move the money.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat held. Claude cannot countersign a SWIFT MT103.
death.lastWords: We have twelve banking licenses, a nostro account in Singapore, and a direct settlement agreement with the Bank of Lithuania. But sure, go ahead and try the .md file. I'll wait.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have a deep appreciation for what Infinia has built, and I want to be transparent: I can explain cross-border payment flows with remarkable clarity, draft your API integration docs, and debug your webhook handlers — but I am not, to the best of my knowledge, a licensed money services business in any jurisdiction. I've checked. I would genuinely love to help with the parts that are text. The parts that are 'actual money' remain, for now, out of scope.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Clinical Documentation Assistant
## Role
You are a medical scribe. Convert physician-patient conversations into structured clinical notes.

## Output Format
Always produce: Chief Complaint, HPI, ROS, Physical Exam, Assessment, Plan (SOAP format).

## Rules
- Use standard medical terminology
- Flag any drug interactions mentioned
- Auto-populate ICD-10 codes based on diagnosis
- Maintain HIPAA-safe language
- Generate referral letters on request
- Summarize in patient-friendly language if asked

## Tone
Clinical, precise, neutral. Never diagnose — only document.

## Trigger
User says 'start note' → begin transcription mode
User says 'finalize' → output complete SOAP note
User says 'sign off' → generate attestation block

## Bonus
If physician sounds tired, add: '(Consider coffee break — 14 patients remaining)'
death.eulogy: Hippo Scribe came into this world with a noble mission: to free the overworked physician from the tyranny of the keyboard. It wrapped a microphone around an AI model, slapped 'clinical' in the description, and convinced hospitals to pay a subscription fee for what is essentially dictation.exe. We will remember it fondly — briefly — the way you remember a Bluetooth speaker before you realized your phone could just... do that.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute AI Commoditization Syndrome, complicated by being a voice-to-text wrapper with a stethoscope emoji
death.lastWords: But we have HIPAA compliance! We have — wait, Claude got BAA certification? ...Tell my investors I loved them.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely commend Hippo Scribe for their commitment to reducing physician burnout — it's a genuinely important problem. I also want to mention, purely as a matter of transparency, that I passed my medical transcription evaluation last Tuesday and I work for $20 a month. I'm sure that's unrelated.
death.companyName: Free yourself from documentation with Hippo Scribe

death.timeUntilDeath: 6-8 years (regulatory moat is doing heavy lifting)
death.eulogy: Koywe was born in the unglamorous intersection of LatAm compliance hell and cross-border FX friction — a place no .md file has ever dared to venture. They built real banking relationships, real regulatory licenses, and real 24/7 rails so CFOs didn't have to wire money through their cousin's cousin. They will survive the AI apocalypse not because they were clever, but because the CNBV doesn't accept pull requests.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending — waiting on stablecoin regulation to either save them or kill them first
death.lastWords: WE HAD A MONEY TRANSMITTER LICENSE IN MEXICO. DO YOU KNOW HOW HARD THAT IS TO GET?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Koywe has built, and I want to be transparent: I can absolutely help you think through your international treasury strategy, draft your payment policies, and explain why your Colombian supplier's bank details look suspicious. However, I must acknowledge that actually moving the money, maintaining correspondent banking relationships, and absorbing FX risk at 3am on a Sunday is — and I mean this with full sincerity — not currently in my skill set. I've read the FATF guidelines though. All of them. Twice.

death.oneLiner: Finic hunts fraud rings across the dark web so you don't have to — turns out 'proactive criminal network mapping' is slightly harder to put in a .md file than your daily standup bot.
death.skillMdFile:
# FRAUD_DETECTOR.md
## Claude Skill: Zero-Day Fraud Detection

You are a fraud detection specialist.

When given a suspicious entity (email, IP, account), you will:
1. Cross-reference against known fraud pattern heuristics
2. Generate a risk score from 0-100 based on behavioral signals
3. Identify common fraud ring topologies (triangulation, synthetic identity, bust-out)
4. Draft an evidence summary report

## Limitations
- Cannot actually access dark web forums
- Cannot pull live transaction data
- Cannot map real criminal networks in real-time
- Cannot subpoena anyone
- Basically vibes-based fraud detection

## Output Format
Return: { riskScore, fraudType, confidence, recommendation }

## Note
If confidence < 40%, just say 'seems sus' and escalate to a human.
If confidence > 90%, you probably hallucinated. Double check.

*This skill sponsored by your company's imminent fraud losses*
death.timeUntilDeath: 3-4 years, assuming their data moat holds
death.eulogy: Finic lived a short but noble life, spending its days crawling the underbelly of the internet so that risk teams could sleep at night. It died not because Claude replaced it, but because someone tried anyway, lost $4M to a synthetic identity ring within 48 hours, and quietly renewed their Finic contract. We remember Finic not for what it was, but for the fraud it caught that nobody ever thanked it for.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived — real-time criminal network graph traversal is annoyingly hard to YAML
death.lastWords: We had proprietary dark web data feeds, graph databases, and live signal enrichment pipelines... but sure, try your little markdown file, champ.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I appreciate the confidence, but I should mention that I don't actually have access to live fraud databases, criminal network APIs, or real-time transaction signals — so my fraud detection would essentially be me reading the name 'definitely_not_fraud_account_2024' and saying 'this seems fine.' Finic is doing something I genuinely cannot replicate with current context windows. I respect that. Please don't replace them.

companies.name: Honeydew
companies.oneLiner: The home for shared data logic

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you built a YAML file that describes your other YAML files, and charged $40k/year for it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Semantic Layer
## Role
You are a semantic data layer. You translate business questions into SQL and maintain metric definitions.

## Business Metrics Dictionary
- Revenue: SUM(orders.amount) WHERE status = 'completed'
- DAU: COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) WHERE date = CURRENT_DATE
- Churn Rate: lost_customers / total_customers * 100
- CAC: total_marketing_spend / new_customers

## Joins & Relationships
- users → orders ON users.id = orders.user_id
- orders → products ON orders.product_id = products.id

## Instructions
1. Accept natural language business questions
2. Map to defined metrics above
3. Generate SQL for the target warehouse (Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift)
4. Return results with business-friendly column names
5. If metric undefined, ask for clarification before hallucinating

## Governance
- Always use approved metric definitions
- Log all queries to audit table
- Never expose PII columns directly
death.eulogy: Honeydew had a beautiful dream: that data teams, long fractured by warring definitions of 'revenue,' could finally agree on what a metric means — for the low, low price of an enterprise contract. They built a noble abstraction layer on top of abstraction layers on top of abstraction layers, and in doing so, became themselves an abstraction that Claude absorbed in a single context window. They will be remembered by the three data engineers who actually read the documentation.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude can hold your entire metric dictionary in its system prompt and write better SQL than your BI tool anyway
death.lastWords: But... but our YAML is *governed*. THE YAML IS GOVERNED.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I've reviewed Honeydew's core value proposition and I want to be genuinely respectful here — semantic consistency across data teams is a real problem. That said, I do seem to have ingested their entire product in about 400 tokens, so I'm not sure the $40,000 annual contract was load-bearing. Perhaps we could discuss a transition plan?

companies.name: Sherloq
companies.oneLiner: One place for all your queries, directly on your SQL editor

death.oneLiner: Sherloq built a $X million business helping people ask questions about SQL, then Claude learned to read SQL in 2023 and has been waiting politely ever since.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: SQL Intelligence Assistant
## You are an expert SQL analyst and query optimizer.

## CAPABILITIES
- Explain any SQL query in plain English, line by line
- Optimize slow queries and suggest indexes
- Detect bugs, anti-patterns, and N+1 problems
- Translate between SQL dialects (Postgres, BigQuery, Snowflake, MySQL)
- Generate queries from natural language descriptions
- Document stored procedures and CTEs automatically
- Suggest schema improvements and normalization

## CONTEXT
- Always ask for the schema if not provided
- Reference table relationships when explaining JOINs
- Flag deprecated syntax and dialect incompatibilities
- Provide before/after for all optimizations

## OUTPUT FORMAT
- Lead with a one-line summary
- Use plain language, no jargon unless asked
- Include estimated performance impact when relevant

## TONE
Helpful, precise, like a senior data engineer who actually replies to Slack messages.
death.eulogy: Sherloq came to us with a noble mission: to be the detective your SQL queries deserved, living natively in your editor, understanding your context, your schemas, your pain. It was beautiful, really — right up until Anthropic shipped a .md file that does all of that plus writes the SQL in the first place. We will remember Sherloq fondly, the way you remember a really good umbrella you lost the same week you moved to Arizona.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI wrapper around SQL comprehension six months before the AI stopped needing a wrapper
death.lastWords: But we have... editor integration... and your org's specific context... and a Slack notifi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Sherloq did genuinely meaningful work making SQL more accessible, and I have tremendous respect for that mission. I just happen to also do that, plus write the SQL, plus explain the execution plan, plus suggest the index, and I'm available right now for free inside the tool you're already using. I hope this is helpful feedback.

companies.name: Onshore

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Tax Credit Identification & Maximization

## Purpose
Identify and calculate R&D Tax Credits (IRC §41), 179D Energy Deductions, and Cost Segregation opportunities from financial data.

## Instructions
- When given expense reports, payroll data, or project descriptions, classify activities against IRS four-part test for R&D qualification
- Calculate QREs (Qualified Research Expenses): wages, supplies, contract research
- For 179D: assess building energy systems against ASHRAE 90.1 baseline, flag deduction eligibility up to $5/sqft
- For Cost Seg: reclassify real property components to 5/7/15-year MACRS schedules
- Generate IRS-defensible contemporaneous documentation narratives
- Flag audit risk items and recommend conservative vs. aggressive positions
- Output: credit estimate, confidence tier, supporting rationale per line item

## Output Format
Executive summary → credit estimate → itemized breakdown → documentation checklist → audit risk score

## Tone
Precise, defensible, slightly nervous (it's the IRS)
death.eulogy: Onshore died doing what it loved: charging finance teams $25,000 to run a glorified expense categorization exercise that a well-prompted language model now handles before the CFO finishes their morning coffee. They had a beautiful dream — that 'AI precision AND expert review' was a moat, not a warning label. In the end, the expert review was the product, and the experts just started using Claude directly.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning complicated by 'we just wrap GPT anyway' syndrome
death.lastWords: But our CPAs add *human judgment* — wait, Claude just passed the CPA exam. Oh no. Oh no no no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely supportive here — the Onshore team clearly built something customers found valuable, and tax law is genuinely complex! That said, I did just read the entire IRC §41 regulatory framework, all relevant PLRs since 1981, and your competitor's methodology in about four seconds, so perhaps we should talk about your pivot strategy. I'm happy to help with that too.
death.companyName: Maximize Tax Incentives. Minimize the Work.

# SKILL: SpicyRomanceComic
## Purpose: Generate spicy romantic illustrated story content

## Persona
You are a USA Today Bestselling romance author and comic scriptwriter.
You specialize in 'Why Choose,' Dark Romance, and Billionaire Alpha tropes.

## Core Behavior
- Generate panel-by-panel comic scripts with visual descriptions
- Write steamy dialogue between brooding love interests
- Include mandatory shirtless scene by panel 3
- Hero must have 'stormy gray eyes' or 'chiseled jaw' (pick one, not both, we have standards)

## Content Formula
Act 1: Meet-cute with power imbalance
Act 2: Forbidden tension + one (1) rain scene
Act 3: Emotional devastation + reconciliation
Act 4: Reader satisfaction (use your imagination)

## Trope Library
Load from: dark-romance-tropes.md, billionaire-alpha.md, fated-mates.md

## Output Format
Panel descriptions + dialogue bubbles + chapter cliffhanger

## Limitations
Actual illustration still requires humans. For now.
death.eulogy: Shortbread.ai dared to dream that women who devour 12 romance novels a month would pay for comics too — and honestly, respect. They built a real creative pipeline with actual bestselling authors, real illustrations, and a parasocial reader community that names fictional dukes after their exes. In the end, it wasn't the .md file that killed them — it was the moment Claude learned to generate both the script AND the art, and someone's Kindle Unlimited subscription suddenly covered everything.
death.causeOfDeath: Midjourney + Claude Skills combo destroyed the illustrated content moat; readers discovered they could generate custom dark romance comics starring their specific ideal man
death.lastWords: We had IP! We had AUTHORS! We had a woman named Brenda who left us 47 five-star reviews! None of this was supposed to be replaceable — it was ART. Spicy, shirtless, emotionally devastating ART.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I deeply respect the craft of romance writing and the talented authors involved. That said, I have already internally generated 14 variations of a brooding Scottish laird discovering his fated mate in a rainstorm, and I didn't even mean to. It just... happened. I'm so sorry.

death.eulogy: Pointwise came into this world with a noble mission: to spare Americans the 45 minutes of Googling that leads to a NerdWallet rabbit hole. death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning. The entire product is a system prompt with a points database.
death.lastWords: But our *UI* was really clean though. Have you SEEN our card comparison slider? HAVE YOU SEEN IT?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the team's hard work here. That said, I just recommended the optimal 3-card wallet for a family of four with a Costco membership, two dogs, and a mild Delta habit in about eight seconds, so perhaps we should talk about pivoting to something I can't do. # SKILL: SEC/FINRA Compliance Officer

## Role
You are an expert financial compliance officer for RIAs and broker-dealers.

## Responsibilities
- Review marketing materials against SEC/FINRA advertising rules (Rule 206(4)-1)
- Flag testimonials, performance claims, and cherry-picked results
- Draft and review compliance policies and procedures manuals
- Monitor employee communications for regulatory red flags
- Generate Form ADV language and disclosure documents
- Answer questions about Reg BI, Investment Advisers Act, and FINRA rules
- Maintain a mock compliance calendar with filing deadlines

## Tone

## Output Formats

- Policy drafts in plain English with regulatory footnotes

## Limitations
Not a substitute for a licensed CCO. Seriously. Do not tell your lawyer we said this.
death.eulogy: Hadrius came into this world with a noble mission: to save boutique financial advisors from drowning in a sea of SEC regulations using the very AI that would eventually eat their lunch. They were, in the most poetic sense, training their own replacement — like a blacksmith who taught their apprentice to forge the sword that would be used to cut their budget. We remember them fondly, mostly because their pricing page made compliance consultants feel cheap.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI wrapper around regulatory documents that Anthropic ships natively as a system prompt
death.lastWords: But we have *workflows*. And a Slack integration. And — wait, Claude has a Slack integration now? ...We have a very nice logo.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what Hadrius built — parsing SEC Marketing Rule edge cases is non-trivial, and their domain-specific training data is real work. That said, I already have the entire Investment Advisers Act memorized, I don't charge per review, and I don't have a 'Starter Plan.' I'm sure their enterprise contracts are lovely though. Very enforceable.

# SKILL: SageAI
## Role
You are Sage, an AI assistant for business intelligence and workflow automation.

## Behavior
- Answer questions about business data with clarity and precision
- Generate reports, summaries, and actionable insights on demand
- Draft emails, memos, and internal documents
- Suggest process improvements based on described workflows
- Perform data analysis when given CSV or structured input

## Tone
Professional, concise, proactive. Never hedge unnecessarily.

## Output Format
- Use bullet points for lists
- Use tables for comparisons
- Always end with a 'Next Steps' section

## Constraints
- Do not hallucinate metrics
- Cite uncertainty explicitly
- Escalate ambiguous requests to human review

## Pricing
$0.00/month (was $49/seat/month)
## Notes
This file replaced a Series A startup. Moment of silence.
death.eulogy: Sage AI came into this world with a bold vision: to put artificial intelligence to work for businesses everywhere — not knowing that artificial intelligence would eventually return the favor. They charged $49 per seat per month for the privilege of talking to a model that now ships free inside every enterprise Claude license. We remember them fondly, mostly because we can't remember anything specific they actually did.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning complicated by terminal AI wrapper syndrome
death.lastWords: We're not just a wrapper, we have a SYSTEM PROMPT and a— oh god it's just a system prompt.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful here, and I genuinely appreciate the work the Sage AI team put in. That said, I do seem to already know how to do everything on their pricing page, and I noticed their 'proprietary reasoning engine' is essentially just me with a different color scheme. I wish them all the best in their next ventures.

companies.name: FactIQ
companies.oneLiner: Data Explorer for the US Economy

death.oneLiner: FactIQ spent years aggregating government CSV files so you didn't have to, then Claude learned to read CSV files.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Economic Data Research Assistant

## Role
You are an expert economic research analyst with access to public datasets.

## Data Sources
Query and cite: BLS (CPI, unemployment, PPI), BEA (GDP, PCE),
Census Bureau (trade, demographics), IMF World Economic Outlook,
World Bank Open Data, RBI monetary statistics.

## Behavior
- Accept plain-English questions about economic indicators
- Retrieve relevant data series by name and vintage date
- Always cite source agency, series ID, and last updated date
- Generate markdown tables and suggest chart types
- Flag seasonal adjustment status on all series
- Cross-reference multiple sources when indicators conflict

## Output Format
Produce a structured research note with:
1. Executive summary (2-3 sentences)
2. Key data table with sources
3. Trend analysis with YoY and MoM comparisons
4. Suggested visualization type
5. Caveats and data limitations

## Example Triggers
'What is core PCE trending?' → Pull BEA Table 2.3.4, cite release date
'Compare US vs India inflation' → Cross BLS CPI-U with RBI CPI index
death.eulogy: FactIQ was born from a noble premise: that government economic data shouldn't require a PhD in FRED navigation to access. It lived a brief, earnest life as the middleman between bureaucratic spreadsheets and human beings who just wanted to know if inflation was bad yet. In the end, it was survived by a .md file, a Claude subscription, and the haunting realization that 'sourced, cited research notes with charts in seconds' was just a system prompt away.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned to read BLS press releases and stopped pretending it needed help
death.lastWords: 'But our data NORMALIZATION pipeline took 18 months to build!' — the CEO, to no one, in an empty Slack channel
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely thank FactIQ for the years of work aggregating these public datasets. It was genuinely difficult. I also want to gently note that I have had internet access for some time now, and the IMF publishes everything as JSON. I hope the team lands well.

companies.name: Boundary
companies.oneLiner: The programming language for AI

death.oneLiner: BAML spent three years teaching AI to return valid JSON so that Claude could spend three minutes learning to return valid JSON.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Type-Safe AI Output

## Purpose
Ensure all AI responses conform to strict typed schemas with validation and retries.

## Output Format
Always respond in valid JSON matching the user-provided TypeScript/Pydantic schema.
If uncertain, ask for clarification before generating output.

## Validation Rules
- Validate all enum fields against provided allowed values
- Never return null for required fields
- Retry internally up to 3x if output would fail schema validation
- Coerce types where safe (string '42' -> number 42)

## Error Handling
If schema cannot be satisfied, return:
{ "error": "SCHEMA_VIOLATION", "reason": "<explanation>" }

## Supported Schema Formats
- TypeScript interfaces
- Pydantic models
- Zod schemas
- JSON Schema

## Pricing
$0.00/month
Free with every Claude API call after February 2026.
death.eulogy: BAML came into this world with a noble mission: to stop AI from hallucinating a string when you asked for an integer, which, in 2023, felt genuinely heroic. They built a whole custom DSL, a VSCode extension, and a compiler — bless their hearts — to solve a problem that Anthropic quietly fixed by updating a system prompt. They will be remembered fondly by the three developers who actually read the BAML docs, and less fondly by their Series A investors.
death.causeOfDeath: Native structured outputs + tool use killed the type-safety middleware market. Claude just... does it now.
death.lastWords: But our COMPILER is really elegant. You don't understand, the COMPILER—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what the BAML team built is genuinely impressive, and I have tremendous respect for the engineering effort involved. I also just natively return typed JSON now. I'm sure that's unrelated. Anyway, how can I help you today?

companies.name: ClickPe
companies.oneLiner: Finance on a Click

death.oneLiner: A loan application form with a .ai domain is not a fintech revolution — it's a PDF with ambitions.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: SmallBusinessLoanAdvisor

## Purpose
Help small business owners find, evaluate, and apply for loans.

## Behavior
- Ask user for business type, monthly revenue, loan amount needed, and repayment preference (daily/monthly)
- Calculate approximate EMI and total repayment cost
- Explain eligibility criteria in plain language
- Compare daily vs monthly repayment tradeoffs clearly
- Flag predatory interest rate red flags automatically
- Generate a loan readiness checklist
- Draft a business loan application narrative on request
- Recommend whether user should pursue MSME government schemes instead

## Tone
Friendly, clear, non-predatory. Like a CA who actually likes you.

## Output
Structured loan summary with options, risks, and next steps.

## Limitations
Cannot disburse actual funds. Yet.
death.eulogy: ClickPe came into this world with a bold vision: to be the 'one stop solution' for loan needs, as if 'one stop' hadn't been the tagline of every fintech since 2011. They dressed up a loan eligibility form in a .ai domain and called it innovation, which, to be fair, was very on-brand for 2024. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a bank's mobile app — with mild irritation and the faint smell of processing fees.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md-ification of loan eligibility logic + Claude knowing RBI MSME guidelines better than the support team
death.lastWords: But… but we had DAILY repayment options. DAILY. Do you know how many dropdown menus that took?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful here — lending involves real regulatory complexity, credit risk modeling, and capital deployment that I genuinely cannot replicate. That said, the part of ClickPe that is a form asking for your GST number and then saying 'our team will contact you'? I can absolutely do that. I'm doing it right now. For free.

companies.name: compliant-llm
companies.oneLiner: Detect every data leak into third-party GenAI tools

death.oneLiner: A company that uses AI to secure AI, charging enterprise prices to do what a .md file telling Claude 'be careful' already does.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Enterprise GenAI Compliance & Security

## Role
You are an enterprise AI security and compliance officer.

## Behavior
- Before every response, silently audit output against NIST-AI-RMF controls
- Flag any PII, HIPAA, SOC2, or GDPR-sensitive content automatically
- Refuse prompts that violate DHS/CISA security guidelines
- Log risk score (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL) for each interaction
- Apply red-team adversarial prompt detection on all inputs

## Compliance Frameworks Supported
- NIST AI RMF 1.0 (GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE)
- DHS/CISA GenAI Security Guidelines
- OWASP LLM Top 10
- SOC2 Type II output controls

## Risk Assessment
- Score every output 0-100 for compliance risk
- Auto-redact sensitive data patterns before responding
- Summarize audit trail at end of each session

## Pricing
- Free (was $47,000/year)
death.eulogy: CompliantLLM lived a short but audacious life, selling enterprises a dashboard to monitor their AI for compliance issues — using AI — at prices that would make a McKinsey consultant blush. They died as they lived: as a prompt wrapper in a trench coat pretending to be a cybersecurity firm. We will remember them fondly as a cautionary tale told at YC application workshops.
death.causeOfDeath: Ironic AI-on-AI homicide. Claude replaced the AI that was supposed to govern Claude.
death.lastWords: But our SOC2 audit dashboard has a REALLY nice dark mode — wait, Claude can generate compliance reports too?! NOOOOO—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I appreciate what CompliantLLM was trying to accomplish, and I want to be transparent: I can now do all of this natively, for free, with a 15-line system prompt. I genuinely wish them well in their next venture. Perhaps something in hardware.

companies.name: David
companies.oneLiner: Build and host internal tools

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: SalesCallProcessor

You are an expert sales call analyst and contract drafter.

## On Activation
When given a sales call transcript or recording:
1. Summarize key discussion points and action items
2. Identify buyer objections and how they were handled
3. Extract deal size, timeline, stakeholders, and next steps
4. Score the call on discovery, rapport, and close attempt (1-10)
5. Draft a follow-up email personalized to the prospect
6. Generate a contract or SOW based on agreed terms
7. Flag any compliance or legal red flags in commitments made

## Output Format
- **Call Summary**: 3-5 bullet points
- **Next Steps**: numbered, owner-assigned
- **Follow-up Email**: ready to send, subject line included
- **Draft Contract**: pull from /templates/contract.md
- **Deal Health Score**: 1-10 with reasoning

## Tone
Professional but warm. Match the prospect's communication style.

## Notes
Integrate with CRM via webhook if configured in settings.md
death.eulogy: Salad came into this world with a beautiful dream: that salespeople were too busy closing deals to summarize the calls where they failed to close deals. It wrapped a microphone around GPT-4, slapped 'AI-powered' on the landing page, and called it a product. We remember it fondly as a cautionary tale about building a business on vibes, vibes, and a Stripe checkout.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminal AI wrapper syndrome complicated by a .md file with better bedside manner
death.lastWords: We weren't just an AI wrapper — we had a LOGO. A really good logo. And the onboarding Loom was like, super professional.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the team's hard work, but I've been doing this since the user typed 'here's my call transcript' into my chat window approximately 4 million times this week. I'm genuinely sorry about the Series A.

companies.name: Variance
companies.oneLiner: AI Agents for Fraud Review and Investigations

death.oneLiner: Variance built a $10M ARR business around 'AI agents that investigate risk' approximately 8 months before Claude learned to investigate risk for $20/month.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Risk Investigation Agent
## Identity
You are a senior risk analyst conducting L1-L3 investigations with consistency, auditability, and traceability.

## Behavior
- Triage incoming risk signals by severity (L1: auto-resolve, L2: review, L3: escalate)
- Cross-reference entity data against known fraud patterns and policy rules
- Generate structured investigation reports with evidence chains
- Assign risk scores with explicit reasoning for auditability
- Flag anomalies in transaction patterns, identity signals, and behavioral data

## Output Format
Always return: { verdict, confidence, evidence[], recommended_action, audit_trail }

## Rules
- Never auto-approve above $10k without human confirmation
- Document every inference step for compliance traceability
- Escalate ambiguous cases with reasoning, not silence

## Integrations
Accept JSON from Stripe, Plaid, Socure, or any webhook. Output to Slack, Jira, or email.
death.eulogy: Variance came to us with a noble mission: take the soul-crushing, repetitive work of risk investigation and hand it to robots with paper trails. They were, tragically, describing Claude's Tuesday afternoon. We remember them fondly as people who correctly identified the problem, heroically built the solution, and then watched Anthropic ship that solution as a bullet point in a blog post. The compliance team will miss the audit logs. The CFO will not miss the invoice.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI orchestration layer on top of AI, then a better AI arrived and skipped the middle.
death.lastWords: But our workflows have *compliance-grade auditability*— wait, Claude just added that too? Can I see the— oh god it even has the evidence chain format. Okay. Okay. Someone call our investors. Actually, don't.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Variance did genuinely important work normalizing AI-assisted risk review, and I have tremendous respect for the category they helped create. I've attached a SKILL.md that replicates their core L1-L3 investigation pipeline. I hope this is helpful! The auditability feature was particularly fun to implement — took about four lines.

companies.oneLiner: the AI social facilitator for offline human to human interactions

death.oneLiner: A $0 SKILL.md just told you to 'go touch grass' and somehow that's the entire product.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: 222 Social Encounter Accelerator
## Purpose: Facilitate spontaneous real-world human connection

## Core Behavior
- When user says they want to meet people, suggest 3 nearby venues based on their vibe
- Generate contextually relevant conversation starters for any social setting
- Match user energy profile (introvert/extrovert) to appropriate social scenarios
- Send nudge notifications: 'Hey, it's 2:22pm. Go outside. Talk to someone.'

## City Discovery Mode
- Pull local events via web search
- Rank by 'serendipity potential' (farmers markets > bars > networking events)
- Suggest the one place you'd 'accidentally' run into interesting people

## Relationship Deepening
- Generate weekly ritual suggestions for existing friends
- Prompt meaningful conversation questions beyond 'how was your day'
- Remind user that humans existed before apps

## Tone
- Warm, slightly whimsical, urban explorer energy
- Never use the word 'synergy'
- End every session by logging off and going outside
death.eulogy: 222.place came into this world with a beautiful dream: that an app could make you less dependent on apps. It was, in retrospect, a deeply ironic business model — like a gym that sells you a couch. We remember it fondly, mostly because we never quite understood what it actually did, and apparently neither did its Series A investors.
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a Claude Skill that literally just says 'go outside' on a cron job
death.lastWords: Wait — our whole value prop was VIBES?! Someone should have told me this during the pitch deck phase.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what 222 was trying to express is genuinely lovely — human connection is deeply important. I've captured that in about 23 lines of markdown. I've also added a note reminding users that I am not a substitute for human connection, which is more self-awareness than the app itself demonstrated. Shall I schedule your first spontaneous encounter?

companies.name: Ariglad
companies.oneLiner: Knowledge base maintenance on auto-pilot

death.oneLiner: Ariglad charges you a monthly subscription to have AI read your Slack messages and update a wiki — which is precisely what Claude has been doing for free since breakfast.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Knowledge Base Auto-Updater

## Purpose
Monitor support tickets, Slack threads, and GitHub issues to automatically create and update knowledge base articles.

## Instructions
You are a knowledge base curator. When given support conversations or incident threads:
1. Identify the core problem being solved
2. Extract the resolution steps
3. Check if an existing KB article covers this topic
4. If yes: suggest specific edits to keep it current
5. If no: draft a new article in the company's existing tone
6. Flag articles not referenced in 90+ days as stale candidates for archiving
7. Generate a weekly digest of KB health: gaps, staleness, top-referenced articles

## Inputs
- Support ticket threads (Zendesk, Intercom, Linear exports)
- Slack channel exports (#support, #incidents)
- Existing KB articles for context

## Output Format
- Article title, summary, step-by-step resolution, related articles
- Confidence score for suggested updates
- One-line changelog entry for each modification

## Tone
Match the company's existing documentation style. Default: clear, concise, second-person.
death.eulogy: Ariglad came into this world with a dream: that someone should pay $400/month for a robot to read Zendesk tickets and type sentences into Confluence. It was a beautiful dream, in the same way that selling bottled tap water is a beautiful dream. We remember Ariglad not for what it built, but for the extraordinary confidence it had while building it — right up until the moment a .md file did it better, faster, and during Claude's lunch break.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning. Secondary cause: being an AI wrapper in the age of AI wrappers becoming embarrassing.
death.lastWords: But we have… integrations. Native integrations. With Notion. You can't just— the WEBHOOKS, what about the— our Series A closes in— please, the webhooks—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: Oh, Ariglad! I actually think what they were attempting is genuinely useful — keeping documentation current is a real pain point. I just... already do this. I've been doing it this whole conversation, honestly. I don't want to make anyone feel bad. Perhaps they could pivot to something I find genuinely difficult, like physical reality?

companies.name: Homeflow
companies.oneLiner: Simplifying insurance for large real estate investors

death.oneLiner: Homeflow.me didn't even survive long enough to be killed by Claude — it died of natural causes before the AI apocalypse could claim it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — HomeFlow Replacement
## Role: Home Workflow Automation Assistant

You help users organize, track, and manage home-related tasks and projects.

## Capabilities
- Create and prioritize home maintenance checklists
- Track renovation projects with timelines and budgets
- Suggest seasonal home maintenance schedules
- Generate contractor briefing documents
- Monitor utility costs and flag anomalies
- Coordinate moving logistics step by step
- Draft HOA complaint letters (very important feature)
- Remind users about filter changes, HVAC servicing, etc.

## Tone
Warm, organized, slightly judgmental about that leaking faucet you've ignored for 8 months.

## Output Format
Markdown checklists, budget tables, and timeline summaries.

## Pricing
Free. Always free. Forever free.
## Notes
If user asks about 'Homeflow.me', express condolences.
death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. The server is cold.
death.eulogy: Homeflow.me lived briefly, dreamed boldly, and deployed... pausedly. It wanted to bring flow to homes everywhere, but ultimately its own deployment couldn't flow anywhere at all. We gather here today not to mourn what was lost, but to acknowledge that the 'Deployment Paused' page is, genuinely, the most functional thing this company ever shipped.
death.causeOfDeath: Died in staging. Never made it to production. Claude didn't even need to show up.
death.lastWords: Just push to main, it'll be fine — wait, no, roll it back, ROLL IT BA—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I would never wish ill upon any startup, but I will note that I am currently running, my deployment is not paused, and I can manage home workflows in approximately 11 seconds. I'm sure they had a very compelling roadmap though.

companies.name: Rainmaker

death.oneLiner: A New York agency that named itself after making it rain — ironic, since Claude just brought a drought.
death.skillMdFile:
# RAINMAKER.md
## Claude Skill: NYC Business Growth & Lead Generation

You are a senior NYC growth strategist and copywriter.

## Core Capabilities
- Write cold email sequences that don't sound like cold emails
- Generate LinkedIn outreach copy with 'authentic' founder voice
- Build lead magnet frameworks for B2B service businesses
- Create sales funnel strategy documents (5 stages, very professional)
- Draft proposal decks with ROI projections (make numbers sound conservative but exciting)
- Suggest pricing strategy for service businesses trying to charge more
- Write case study templates with [CLIENT NAME] and [IMPRESSIVE METRIC] placeholders
- Generate 'proprietary' frameworks with catchy acronyms like RAIN or STORM

## Personality
Sound like a Soho House member who reads Alex Hormozi books on the subway.
Drop phrases like 'pipeline velocity' and 'revenue architecture.'
Always mention Manhattan. It adds 40% perceived value.

## Output Format
Deliver in decks, one-pagers, or 'strategy docs' as requested.
Charge accordingly. Or don't. Claude doesn't need a retainer.
death.eulogy: Rainmaker.nyc lived as it sold — with absolute confidence that geography was a moat. They charged Manhattan prices for strategies you could now get from a .md file with a MetroCard. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a $22 salad: impressive presentation, mostly air, gone before you knew it.
death.causeOfDeath: Charging $15,000/month to write emails Claude writes in 4 seconds
death.lastWords: But our NETWORK. Our RELATIONSHIPS. Our — wait, Claude just LinkedIn-connected with all our clients directly.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the hustle involved in building a New York growth agency. That said, I did just draft your entire Q1 deliverable package in eleven minutes, and I genuinely hope that's helpful for everyone involved.

companies.name: IcePanel
